nominal_value.source: TEP-EFA pipeline weighted mean from flyby anomaly analysis
nominal_value.derivation: β = 1×10⁻⁴ is the nominal coupling constant for TEP scalar field; this value is derived from the ensemble weighted mean of flyby anomaly fits; ±50% uncertainty accounts for systematic uncertainty in GNSS calibration and heterogeneity across flybys

nominal_value.source: GNSS atomic clock correlation analysis (Paper 6, UCD)
nominal_value.derivation: λ_TEP = 4146 km is the characteristic relaxation length from GNSS atomic clock correlation analysis; ±15% uncertainty from the GNSS calibration represents the precision of the clock correlation measurement

nominal_value.source: UCD soliton formula with GNSS-derived ρ_T = 20 g/cm³
nominal_value.derivation: S_⊕ = 0.349 is the characteristic suppression factor from UCD soliton model: R_sol = (3M/4πρ_T)^(1/3) ≈ 4146 km, S_⊕ = (R_earth - R_sol)/R_earth; ±15% uncertainty from GNSS calibration of ρ_T

nominal_value.source: Theoretical coupling strength from Yogyakarta v0.1
nominal_value.derivation: b_disf = 0.5 represents the relative strength of disformal to conformal coupling; this value is calibrated from the Cassini cancellation regime where disformal term partially cancels gradient term; ±50% uncertainty accounts for potential variations in the disformal coupling function B(φ)
J2 = 0.00108263 is Earth's quadrupole moment coefficient from geodetic measurements; ±0.1% uncertainty represents the precision of geodetic measurements
J3 = -2.54×10⁻⁶ is Earth's octupole moment coefficient from geodetic measurements; ±1% uncertainty represents the precision of geodetic measurements

beta.data_source: TEP-EFA pipeline weighted mean from flyby anomaly analysis
beta.derivation: β = 1×10⁻⁴ is the nominal coupling constant for TEP scalar field; this value is derived from the ensemble weighted mean of flyby anomaly fits; ±50% uncertainty accounts for systematic uncertainty in GNSS calibration and heterogeneity across flybys
beta.recommended_action: Reduce uncertainty through additional flyby measurements and independent GNSS calibration validation

lambda_tep_km.data_source: GNSS atomic clock correlation analysis (Paper 6, UCD)
lambda_tep_km.derivation: λ_TEP = 4146 km is the characteristic relaxation length from GNSS atomic clock correlation analysis; ±15% uncertainty from the GNSS calibration represents the precision of the clock correlation measurement
lambda_tep_km.recommended_action: Improve precision through longer GNSS time series and multi-network analysis

characteristic_suppression.data_source: UCD soliton formula with GNSS-derived ρ_T = 20 g/cm³
characteristic_suppression.derivation: S_⊕ = 0.349 is the characteristic suppression factor from UCD soliton model: R_sol = (3M/4πρ_T)^(1/3) ≈ 4146 km, S_⊕ = (R_earth - R_sol)/R_earth; ±15% uncertainty from GNSS calibration of ρ_T
characteristic_suppression.recommended_action: Validate against independent geophysical measurements of field gradients

disformal_coupling.data_source: Theoretical coupling strength from Yogyakarta v0.1
disformal_coupling.derivation: b_disf = 0.5 represents the relative strength of disformal to conformal coupling; this value is calibrated from the Cassini cancellation regime where disformal term partially cancels gradient term; ±50% uncertainty accounts for potential variations in the disformal coupling function B(φ)
disformal_coupling.recommended_action: Empirically constrain through high-velocity flyby measurements and disformal regime tests

j2.derivation: J2 = 0.00108263 is Earth's quadrupole moment coefficient from geodetic measurements; ±0.1% uncertainty represents the precision of geodetic measurements

j3.derivation: J3 = -2.54×10⁻⁶ is Earth's octupole moment coefficient from geodetic measurements; ±1% uncertainty represents the precision of geodetic measurements
